query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 107â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling

 120â†’public class IndentationRule :
 121â†’    StandardRule(
 122â†’        id = "indent",
 123â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 124â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 125â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 126â†’                ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 127â†’                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 128â†’            ),
 129â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 130â†’                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 131â†’                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 132â†’            ),
 133â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 134â†’                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 135â†’                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 136â†’            ),
 137â†’        ),
 138â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 139â†’            CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 140â†’            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 141â†’            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 142â†’        ),
 143â†’    ) {
 144â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 145â†’    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 146â†’
 147â†’    private var line = 1
 148â†’
 149â†’    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 150â†’
 151â†’    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 152â†’
 153â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 154â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 155â†’        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 156â†’            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 157â†’            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 158â†’        )
 159â†’        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 160â†’            stopTraversalOfAST()
 161â†’        }
 162â†’    }
 163â†’
 164â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 165â†’        node: ASTNode,
 166â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 167â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 168â†’    ) {
 169â†’        if (node.isRoot()) {
 170â†’            // File should not start with a whitespace
 171â†’            node
 172â†’                .nextLeaf()
 173â†’                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 174â†’                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 175â†’                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 176â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
 177â†’                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 178â†’                    }
 179â†’                }
 180â†’            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 181â†’        }
 182â†’
 183â†’        when {
 184â†’            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 185â†’                line++
 186â†’                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 187â†’                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 188â†’                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 189â†’                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 190â†’                    )
 191â†’                }
 192â†’                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 193â†’            }
 194â†’
 195â†’            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 196â†’                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 197â†’                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 198â†’                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 199â†’                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 200â†’                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 201â†’                startIndentContext(
 202â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 203â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 204â†’                )
 205â†’
 206â†’            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 207â†’                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 208â†’
 209â†’            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 210â†’                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 211â†’                startIndentContext(node)
 212â†’
 213â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 214â†’                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 215â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 216â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ->
 217â†’                startIndentContext(node)
 218â†’
 219â†’            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 220â†’                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 221â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 222â†’                startIndentContext(
 223â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 224â†’                    childIndent = "",
 225â†’                )
 226â†’
 227â†’            node.elementType == IF ->
 228â†’                visitIf(node)
 229â†’
 230â†’            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 231â†’                visitLbrace(node)
 232â†’
 233â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 234â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 235â†’                startIndentContext(
 236â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 237â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 238â†’                )
 239â†’
 240â†’            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 241â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 242â†’                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 243â†’
 244â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 245â†’                visitValueParameter(node)
 246â†’
 247â†’            node.elementType == FUN ->
 248â†’                visitFun(node)
 249â†’
 250â†’            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 251â†’                visitClass(node)
 252â†’
 253â†’            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 254â†’                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 255â†’
 256â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 257â†’                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 258â†’
 259â†’            node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION ||
 260â†’                node.elementType == SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION ||
 261â†’                node.elementType == USER_TYPE -> {
 262â†’                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 263â†’                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 264â†’                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 265â†’                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 266â†’                ) {
 267â†’                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 268â†’                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 269â†’                    //    .bar[0] {
 270â†’                    //        "foobar"
 271â†’                    //    }
 272â†’                    startIndentContext(
 273â†’                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 274â†’                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 275â†’                    )
 276â†’                } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != node.elementType) {
 277â†’                    startIndentContext(node)
 278â†’                }
 279â†’            }
 280â†’
 281â†’            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 282â†’                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 283â†’                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 284â†’
 285â†’            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 286â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 287â†’                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 288â†’
 289â†’            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 290â†’                visitWhen(node)
 291â†’
 292â†’            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 293â†’                visitWhenEntry(node)
 294â†’
 295â†’            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 296â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 297â†’                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 298â†’
 299â†’            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 300â†’                visitKdoc(node)
 301â†’
 302â†’            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 303â†’                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 304â†’                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 305â†’
 306â†’            node.elementType == FOR ||
 307â†’                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 308â†’                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 309â†’
 310â†’            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 311â†’                visitLBracket(node)
 312â†’
 313â†’            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 314â†’                visitNullableType(node)
 315â†’
 316â†’            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 317â†’                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 318â†’
 319â†’            node.elementType == TRY ->
 320â†’                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 321â†’
 322â†’            else -> {
 323â†’                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 324â†’            }
 325â†’        }
 326â†’    }

 328â†’    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 329â†’        node
 330â†’            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 331â†’            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 332â†’                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 333â†’                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 334â†’                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 335â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 336â†’
 337â†’                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 338â†’                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 339â†’                    startIndentContext(
 340â†’                        fromAstNode = node,
 341â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 342â†’                        childIndent = "",
 343â†’                    )
 344â†’                }
 345â†’            }
 346â†’    }

 348â†’    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 349â†’        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 350â†’        node
 351â†’            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 352â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 353â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 354â†’                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 355â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 356â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 357â†’            }
 358â†’
 359â†’        node
 360â†’            .findChildByType(THEN)
 361â†’            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 362â†’            ?.nextLeaf()
 363â†’            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 364â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 365â†’                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 366â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 367â†’                    childIndent = "",
 368â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369â†’            }
 370â†’        node
 371â†’            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 372â†’            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 373â†’            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 374â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 375â†’                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 376â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 377â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 378â†’            }
 379â†’        startIndentContext(
 380â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 381â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 382â†’            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 383â†’        )
 384â†’    }

 386â†’    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 387â†’        // Outer indent context
 388â†’        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
 389â†’            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 390â†’        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 391â†’        startIndentContext(
 392â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 393â†’            toAstNode = rbrace,
 394â†’            firstChildIndent = "",
 395â†’            lastChildIndent = "",
 396â†’        )
 397â†’
 398â†’        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 399â†’        node
 400â†’            .treeParent
 401â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 402â†’            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 403â†’            ?.let { arrow ->
 404â†’                startIndentContext(
 405â†’                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 406â†’                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 407â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 408â†’                )
 409â†’                startIndentContext(
 410â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 411â†’                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 412â†’                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 413â†’                )
 414â†’            }
 415â†’    }

 417â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 418â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 419â†’            // val fieldExample =
 420â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 421â†’            //              paramA,
 422â†’            //              paramB,
 423â†’            //              paramC ->
 424â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 425â†’            //      }
 426â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 427â†’        } else {
 428â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 429â†’            // val fieldExample =
 430â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 431â†’            //                     paramB,
 432â†’            //                     paramC ->
 433â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 434â†’            //     }
 435â†’            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 436â†’                ?.let { callExpression ->
 437â†’                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 438â†’                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 439â†’                            " { "
 440â†’                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 441â†’                }
 442â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 443â†’        }
 444â†’
 445â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 446â†’        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 447â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 448â†’            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 449â†’
 450â†’    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 451â†’        startIndentContext(
 452â†’            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 453â†’            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 454â†’            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 455â†’            childIndent = "",
 456â†’        )
 457â†’    }

 459â†’    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 460â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 461â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 462â†’        node
 463â†’            .findChildByType(EQ)
 464â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 465â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 466â†’                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 467â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 468â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 469â†’            }
 470â†’
 471â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 472â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 473â†’            //     fun process(
 474â†’            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 475â†’            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 476â†’            //     ): List<Output>
 477â†’            node
 478â†’                .findChildByType(COLON)
 479â†’                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 480â†’                    nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 481â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 482â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 483â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 484â†’                }
 485â†’        }
 486â†’
 487â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 488â†’        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 489â†’        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 490â†’            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 491â†’            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 492â†’        ) {
 493â†’            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 494â†’                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 495â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 496â†’            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 497â†’        } else {
 498â†’            startIndentContext(
 499â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 500â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 501â†’                childIndent = "",
 502â†’            )
 503â†’        }
 504â†’    }

 506â†’    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 507â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 508â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 509â†’        val eqOrBlock =
 510â†’            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 511â†’                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 512â†’        eqOrBlock?.let {
 513â†’            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 514â†’                fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 515â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 516â†’            ).prevCodeLeaf()
 517â†’        }
 518â†’
 519â†’        node
 520â†’            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 521â†’            ?.let { typeReference ->
 522â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 523â†’                    fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 524â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 525â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 526â†’            }
 527â†’
 528â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 529â†’        startIndentContext(
 530â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 531â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 532â†’            childIndent = "",
 533â†’        )
 534â†’    }

 536â†’    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 537â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 538â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 539â†’        node
 540â†’            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 541â†’            ?.let { where ->
 542â†’                val typeConstraintList =
 543â†’                    requireNotNull(
 544â†’                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 545â†’                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 546â†’                require(
 547â†’                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 548â†’                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 549â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 550â†’                    fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 551â†’                    toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 552â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 553â†’            }
 554â†’        node
 555â†’            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 556â†’            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 557â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 558â†’                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 559â†’                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 560â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 561â†’            }
 562â†’
 563â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 564â†’        startIndentContext(
 565â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 566â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 567â†’            childIndent = "",
 568â†’        )
 569â†’    }

 571â†’    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {

 591â†’    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 592â†’        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 593â†’            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 594â†’            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 595â†’            val conditionIndentContext =
 596â†’                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 597â†’            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 598â†’            startIndentContext(
 599â†’                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 600â†’                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 601â†’                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 602â†’                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 603â†’            )
 604â†’            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 605â†’        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 606â†’            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 607â†’        ) {
 608â†’            startIndentContext(node)
 609â†’        }
 610â†’    }

 619â†’    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {

 629â†’            }

 631â†’            fromAstNode = node,

 636â†’    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {

 642â†’                        ""

 900â†’    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 901â†’        node: ASTNode,
 902â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 903â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 904â†’    ) {
 905â†’        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 906â†’            return
 907â†’        }
 908â†’
 909â†’        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 910â†’        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 911â†’        val text = node.text
 912â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 913â†’        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 914â†’            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 915â†’                emit(
 916â†’                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 917â†’                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 918â†’                    true,
 919â†’                )
 920â†’            } else {
 921â†’                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 922â†’                // been emitted.
 923â†’            }
 924â†’            LOGGER.trace {
 925â†’                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 926â†’                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 927â†’            }
 928â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 929â†’                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 930â†’                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 931â†’                )
 932â†’            }
 933â†’        } else {
 934â†’            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 935â†’        }
 936â†’    }

 938â†’    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 939â†’        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 940â†’        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 941â†’            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 942â†’            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 943â†’        }
 944â†’
 945â†’        nextLeaf
 946â†’            ?.parent({ it.psi is PsiComment }, strict = false)
 947â†’            ?.let { comment ->
 948â†’                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 949â†’                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 950â†’                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 951â†’                }
 952â†’                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 953â†’                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 954â†’                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 955â†’                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 956â†’                    return true
 957â†’                }
 958â†’            }
 959â†’        return false
 960â†’    }

 967â†’    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
 968â†’        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 969â†’        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 970â†’        val adjustedChildIndent =
 971â†’            when {
 972â†’                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
 973â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
 974â†’                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
 975â†’
 976â†’                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
 977â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
 978â†’                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
 979â†’
 980â†’                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
 981â†’            }
 982â†’        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
 983â†’    }

 985â†’    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
 986â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 987â†’        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
 988â†’            SPACE -> {
 989â†’                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
 990â†’                    emit(
 991â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 992â†’                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
 993â†’                        true,
 994â†’                    )
 995â†’                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
 996â†’                } else {
 997â†’                    nodeIndent
 998â†’                }
 999â†’            }
1000â†’
1001â†’            TAB -> {
1002â†’                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1003â†’                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1004â†’                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1005â†’                    emit(
1006â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1007â†’                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1008â†’                        true,
1009â†’                    )
1010â†’                    indentConfig
1011â†’                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1012â†’                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1013â†’                } else {
1014â†’                    nodeIndent
1015â†’                }
1016â†’            }
1017â†’        }
1018â†’    }

1031â†’    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1032â†’        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1033â†’        val acceptableTrailingSpaces = when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1034â†’            KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1035â†’                // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1036â†’                // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1037â†’                KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1038â†’            }
1039â†’
1040â†’            TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1041â†’                // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1042â†’                //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1043â†’                //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1044â†’                TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1045â†’            }
1046â†’
1047â†’            else -> ""
1048â†’        }
1049â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1050â†’        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1051â†’            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1052â†’        } else {
1053â†’            ""
1054â†’        }
1055â†’    }

1072â†’    private data class IndentContext(
1073â†’        /**
1074â†’         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1075â†’         */
1076â†’        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1077â†’
1078â†’        /**

1080â†’         * on which the indent context starts.
1081â†’         */

1083â†’
1084â†’        /**

1086â†’         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1087â†’         */
1088â†’        val nodeIndent: String,
1089â†’
1090â†’        /**
1091â†’         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1092â†’         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1093â†’         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1094â†’         */
1095â†’        val firstChildIndent: String,
1096â†’
1097â†’        /**
1098â†’         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1099â†’         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1100â†’         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1101â†’         */
1102â†’        val childIndent: String,
1103â†’
1104â†’        /**
1105â†’         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1106â†’         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1107â†’         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1108â†’         */
1109â†’        val lastChildIndent: String,
1110â†’
1111â†’        /**
1112â†’         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1113â†’         */
1114â†’        val activated: Boolean = false,
1115â†’    ) {
1116â†’        val nodes: String
1117â†’            get() =
1118â†’                fromASTNode
1119â†’                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1120â†’                    ?.leaves()
1121â†’                    .orEmpty()
1122â†’                    .takeWhile {
1123â†’                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1124â†’                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1125â†’                    }
1126â†’                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1127â†’                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1128â†’
1129â†’        fun indent() =
1130â†’            if (activated) {
1131â†’                nodeIndent + childIndent
1132â†’            } else {
1133â†’                nodeIndent
1134â†’            }
1135â†’
1136â†’        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1137â†’    }

1142â†’private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1143â†’    val (prefix, suffix) = if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1144â†’        Pair("[", "]")
1145â†’    } else {
1146â†’        Pair("", "")
1147â†’    }
1148â†’    return prefix
1149â†’        .plus(
1150â†’            this
1151â†’                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1152â†’                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1153â†’        ).plus(suffix)
1154â†’}

1156â†’private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1157â†’    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1158â†’        expectedIndent: String,
1159â†’        node: ASTNode,
1160â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1161â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1162â†’    ) {
1163â†’        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1164â†’        node
1165â†’            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1166â†’            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1167â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1168â†’            ?.let { _ ->
1169â†’                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1170â†’                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1171â†’                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1172â†’                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1173â†’                    emit(
1174â†’                        node.startOffset,
1175â†’                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1176â†’                        false,
1177â†’                    )
1178â†’                    return
1179â†’                }
1180â†’
1181â†’                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()

  9â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12â†’
 13â†’public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14â†’    StandardRule(
 15â†’        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17â†’            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18â†’        ),
 19â†’    ) {
 20â†’    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21â†’
 22â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23â†’        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24â†’    }
 25â†’
 26â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27â†’        node: ASTNode,
 28â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30â†’    ) {
 31â†’        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32â†’            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33â†’            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34â†’            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35â†’            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36â†’                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37â†’            }
 38â†’        }
 39â†’    }
 40â†’
 41â†’    public companion object {
 42â†’        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43â†’        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44â†’
 45â†’        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46â†’            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47â†’
 48â†’            return importsList.map { import ->
 49â†’                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50â†’                    PatternEntry(
 51â†’                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52â†’                        withSubpackages = true,
 53â†’                        hasAlias = false,
 54â†’                    )
 55â†’                } else {
 56â†’                    PatternEntry(
 57â†’                        packageName = import,
 58â†’                        withSubpackages = false,
 59â†’                        hasAlias = false,
 60â†’                    )
 61â†’                }
 62â†’            }
 63â†’        }
 64â†’
 65â†’        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66â†’            { _, value ->
 67â†’                when {
 68â†’                    else -> try {
 69â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70â†’                            value,
 71â†’                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72â†’                        )
 73â†’                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75â†’                            value,
 76â†’                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77â†’                        )
 78â†’                    }
 79â†’                }
 80â†’            }
 81â†’
 82â†’        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
 84â†’                type = PropertyType(
 85â†’                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86â†’                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87â†’                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88â†’                ),
 89â†’                /**
 90â†’                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91â†’                 * it's subpackages.
 92â†’                 *
 93â†’                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94â†’                 */
 95â†’                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96â†’                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97â†’            )
 98â†’    }
 99â†’}
100â†’
101â†’public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId

 3â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 4â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5â†’
 6â†’/**
 7â†’ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8â†’ *
 9â†’ * Properties can be:
10â†’ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11â†’ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12â†’ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13â†’ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14â†’ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15â†’ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16â†’ */
17â†’public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18â†’    /**
19â†’     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20â†’     */
21â†’    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22â†’
23â†’    /**
24â†’     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
25â†’     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26â†’     */
27â†’    public val defaultValue: T,
28â†’
29â†’    /**
30â†’     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31â†’     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32â†’     */
33â†’    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34â†’
35â†’    /**
36â†’     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37â†’     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38â†’     */
39â†’    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40â†’
41â†’    /**
42â†’     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43â†’     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44â†’     */
45â†’    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46â†’
47â†’    /**
48â†’     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49â†’     * below where
50â†’     * ```kotlin
51â†’     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52â†’     *     when {
53â†’     *         property == null ->
54â†’     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55â†’     *         property.isUnset ->
56â†’     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57â†’     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58â†’     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59â†’     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60â†’     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61â†’     *        else ->
62â†’     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63â†’     *     }
64â†’     * }
65â†’     * ```
66â†’     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67â†’     * is used.
68â†’     */
69â†’    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70â†’
71â†’    /**
72â†’     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
73â†’     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
74â†’     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
75â†’     */
76â†’    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
77â†’
78â†’    /**
79â†’     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
80â†’     */
81â†’    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
82â†’
83â†’    /**
84â†’     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
85â†’     */
86â†’    val deprecationError: String? = null,
87â†’
88â†’    /**
89â†’     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
90â†’     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.
91â†’     */
92â†’    public val name: String = type.name,
93â†’)

 54â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 55â†’            // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 56â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 57â†’        ),
 58â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 59â†’            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 60â†’            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 61â†’            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 62â†’            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 63â†’            FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 64â†’        ),
 65â†’    ),
 66â†’    Rule.Experimental {
 67â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 68â†’    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 69â†’    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 70â†’        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 71â†’    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 72â†’
 73â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 74â†’        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[
 75â†’            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 76â†’        ]
 77â†’        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 78â†’        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 79â†’            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 80â†’            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 81â†’        )
 82â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 83â†’    }
 84â†’
 85â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 86â†’        node: ASTNode,
 87â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 88â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 89â†’    ) {
 90â†’        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 91â†’            node
 92â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
 93â†’                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 94â†’                .ifTrue {
 95â†’                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
 96â†’                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
 97â†’                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
 98â†’                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
 99â†’                    // effort to attempt it.
100â†’                    return
101â†’                }
102â†’
103â†’            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
104â†’        }
105â†’    }
106â†’
107â†’    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
108â†’        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
109â†’        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
110â†’        // or
111â†’        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
112â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
113â†’        val startOfBodyBlock =
114â†’            this
115â†’                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
116â†’                ?.firstChildNode
117â†’        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
118â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
119â†’            .childrenBetween(
120â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
121â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
122â†’            )
123â†’    }
124â†’
125â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
126â†’        val funNode = if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
127â†’            this.treeParent
128â†’        } else {
129â†’            this
130â†’        }
131â†’        funNode
132â†’            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
133â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
134â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
135â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
136â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
137â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
138â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
139â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
140â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
141â†’                    ) {
142â†’                        return currentNode
143â†’                    }
144â†’                }
145â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
146â†’            }
147â†’        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
148â†’    }
149â†’
150â†’    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
151â†’        node: ASTNode,
152â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
153â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154â†’    ) {
155â†’        require(node.elementType == FUN)
156â†’
157â†’        val forceMultilineSignature =
158â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
159â†’                node.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine()
160â†’        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
161â†’            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162â†’            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
163â†’            // like below, which are not acceptable:
164â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
165â†’            //     ) = "some-value"
166â†’            //
167â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
168â†’            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
169â†’            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
170â†’            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
171â†’            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
172â†’            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
173â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
174â†’                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
175â†’                // signature needs to be recalculated
176â†’                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
177â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
178â†’            } else {
179â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
180â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
181â†’            }
182â†’        } else {
183â†’            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
184â†’            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
185â†’            // signature.
186â†’            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature = node
187â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
188â†’                .none { it.textContains('\n') }
189â†’            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
190â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
191â†’            } else {
192â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
193â†’            }
194â†’        }
195â†’    }
196â†’
197â†’    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
198â†’        val closingParenthesis =
199â†’            node
200â†’                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
201â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
202â†’        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature = node
203â†’            .functionSignatureNodes()
204â†’            .childrenBetween(
205â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
206â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { false },
207â†’            )
208â†’
209â†’        return node.indent(false).length +
210â†’            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
211â†’    }
212â†’
213â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine(): Boolean =
214â†’        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
215â†’            ?.children()
216â†’            .orEmpty()
217â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
218â†’            .mapNotNull {
219â†’                // If the value parameter contains a modifier then this list is followed by a white space
220â†’                it.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)?.nextSibling { true }
221â†’            }.any { it.textContains('\n') }
222â†’
223â†’    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
224â†’        node: ASTNode,
225â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
226â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
227â†’    ): Int {
228â†’        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
229â†’
230â†’        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
231â†’        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
232â†’        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
233â†’            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
234â†’            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
235â†’    }
236â†’
237â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
238â†’
239â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
240â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
241â†’            .joinTextToString()
242â†’            .length
243â†’
244â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
245â†’        node: ASTNode,
246â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
247â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
248â†’        multiline: Boolean,
249â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
250â†’    ): Int {
251â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
252â†’
253â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
254â†’        val firstParameterInList =
255â†’            valueParameterList
256â†’                .children()
257â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
258â†’
259â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection += if (firstParameterInList == null) {
260â†’            // handle empty parameter list
261â†’            fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
262â†’        } else {
263â†’            fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
264â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
265â†’                fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
266â†’        }
267â†’
268â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
269â†’    }
270â†’
271â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
272â†’        node: ASTNode,
273â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
274â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
275â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
276â†’    ): Int {
277â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
278â†’
279â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
280â†’
281â†’        valueParameterList
282â†’            .children()
283â†’            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
284â†’            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
285â†’                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
286â†’                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
287â†’                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
288â†’            }
289â†’            .firstOrNull()
290â†’            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
291â†’                if (!dryRun) {
292â†’                    emit(
293â†’                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
294â†’                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
295â†’                        true,
296â†’                    )
297â†’                }
298â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
299â†’                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
300â†’                } else {
301â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
302â†’                }
303â†’            }
304â†’
305â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
306â†’    }
307â†’
308â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
309â†’        node: ASTNode,
310â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
311â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
312â†’        multiline: Boolean,
313â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
314â†’    ): Int {
315â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
316â†’
317â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
318â†’        val firstParameterInList =
319â†’            valueParameterList
320â†’                .children()
321â†’                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
322â†’
323â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
324â†’        firstParameter
325â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
326â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
327â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
328â†’                if (multiline) {
329â†’                    val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
330â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
331â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
332â†’                    ) {
333â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
334â†’                            emit(
335â†’                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
336â†’                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
337â†’                                true,
338â†’                            )
339â†’                        }
340â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
341â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
342â†’                        } else {
343â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
344â†’                        }
345â†’                    }
346â†’                } else {
347â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
348â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
349â†’                            emit(
350â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
351â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
352â†’                                true,
353â†’                            )
354â†’                        }
355â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
356â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
357â†’                        } else {
358â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
359â†’                        }
360â†’                    }
361â†’                }
362â†’            }
363â†’
364â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
365â†’    }
366â†’
367â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
368â†’        node: ASTNode,
369â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
371â†’        multiline: Boolean,
372â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
373â†’    ): Int {
374â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
375â†’
376â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
377â†’        val firstParameterInList =
378â†’            valueParameterList
379â†’                .children()
380â†’                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
381â†’
382â†’        valueParameterList
383â†’            .children()
384â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
385â†’            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
386â†’            .forEach { valueParameter ->
387â†’                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
388â†’                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
389â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
390â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
391â†’                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
392â†’                        if (multiline) {
393â†’                            val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
394â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
395â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
396â†’                            ) {
397â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
398â†’                                    emit(
399â†’                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
400â†’                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
401â†’                                        true,
402â†’                                    )
403â†’                                }
404â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
405â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
406â†’                                } else {
407â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
408â†’                                }
409â†’                            }
410â†’                        } else {
411â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
412â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
413â†’                                    emit(
414â†’                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
415â†’                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
416â†’                                        true,
417â†’                                    )
418â†’                                }
419â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
420â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
421â†’                                } else {
422â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
423â†’                                }
424â†’                            }
425â†’                        }
426â†’                    }
427â†’            }
428â†’
429â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
430â†’    }

432â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
433â†’        node: ASTNode,
434â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
435â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436â†’        multiline: Boolean,
437â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
438â†’    ): Int {
439â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
440â†’
441â†’        val newlineAndIndent = node.indent()
442â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
443â†’
444â†’        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
445â†’        closingParenthesis
446â†’            ?.prevSibling { true }
447â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
448â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
449â†’                if (multiline) {
450â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
451â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
452â†’                    ) {
453â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
454â†’                            emit(
455â†’                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
456â†’                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
457â†’                                true,
458â†’                            )
459â†’                        }
460â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
461â†’                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
462â†’                        } else {
463â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
464â†’                        }
465â†’                    }
466â†’                } else {
467â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
468â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
469â†’                    ) {
470â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
471â†’                            emit(
472â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
473â†’                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
474â†’                                true,
475â†’                            )
476â†’                        }
477â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
478â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
479â†’                        } else {
480â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
481â†’                        }
482â†’                    }
483â†’                }
484â†’            }
485â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
486â†’    }

488â†’    private fun fixFunctionBody(
489â†’        node: ASTNode,
490â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
491â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
492â†’        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
493â†’    ) {
494â†’        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
495â†’            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
496â†’        } else {
497â†’            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)
498â†’        }
499â†’    }
500â†’
501â†’    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
502â†’        node: ASTNode,
503â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
504â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
505â†’        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
506â†’    ) {
507â†’        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
508â†’            node
509â†’                .findChildByType(EQ)
510â†’                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
511â†’                ?: return
512â†’        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
513â†’        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
514â†’        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
515â†’
516â†’        val functionBodyExpressionLines = functionBodyExpressionNodes
517â†’            .joinTextToString()
518â†’            .split("\n")
519â†’        functionBodyExpressionLines
520â†’            .firstOrNull()
521â†’            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
522â†’                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
523â†’                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
524â†’                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
525â†’                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
526â†’                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
527â†’                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
528â†’                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
529â†’                                )
530â†’                        } else {
531â†’                            false
532â†’                        }
533â†’                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
534â†’                        emit(
535â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
536â†’                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
537â†’                            true,
538â†’                        )
539â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
540â†’                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
541â†’                        }
542â†’                    }
543â†’                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
544â†’                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
545â†’                ) {
546â†’                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
547â†’                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
548â†’                    ) {
549â†’                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
550â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
551â†’                        ) {
552â†’                            emit(
553â†’                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
554â†’                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
555â†’                                true,
556â†’                            )
557â†’                            if (autoCorrect) {
558â†’                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
559â†’                                    .first()
560â†’                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
561â†’                            }
562â†’                        }
563â†’                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
564â†’                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
565â†’                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
566â†’                    ) {
567â†’                        emit(
568â†’                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
569â†’                            "Newline expected before expression body",
570â†’                            true,
571â†’                        )
572â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
573â†’                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
574â†’                                .first()
575â†’                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
576â†’                        }
577â†’                    }
578â†’                }
579â†’            }
580â†’    }
581â†’
582â†’    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
583â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
584â†’            .childrenBetween(
585â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
586â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
587â†’            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
588â†’            .split("\n")
589â†’            .lastOrNull()
590â†’            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
