1â†’## Annotation formatting
  2â†’
  3â†’Multiple annotations should be on a separate line than the annotated declaration; annotations with parameters should each be on separate lines; annotations should be followed by a space
  4â†’
  5â†’Rule-id: `annotation`
  6â†’
  7â†’## Argument list wrapping
  8â†’
  9â†’Rule-id: `argument-list-wrapping`
 10â†’
 11â†’## Chain wrapping
 12â†’
 13â†’When wrapping chained calls `.`, `?.` and `?:` should be placed on the next line
 14â†’
 15â†’Rule id: `chain-wrapping`
 16â†’
 17â†’## Enum entry
 18â†’
 19â†’Enum entry names should be uppercase underscore-separated names.
 20â†’
 21â†’Rule id: `enum-entry-name-case`
 22â†’
 23â†’## File name
 24â†’
 25â†’Files containing only one toplevel domain should be named according to that element.
 26â†’
 27â†’Rule id: `filename`
 28â†’
 29â†’## Final newline
 30â†’
 31â†’Ensures consistent usage of a newline at the end of each file. 
 32â†’
 33â†’This rule can be configured with `.editorconfig` property [`insert_final_newline`](../configuration-ktlint/#final-newline).
 34â†’
 35â†’Rule id: `final-newline`
 36â†’
 37â†’## Import ordering
 38â†’
 39â†’Imports ordered consistently (see [Custom ktlint EditorConfig properties](#custom-ktlint-specific-editorconfig-properties) for more)
 40â†’
 41â†’Rule id: `import-ordering`
 42â†’
 43â†’## Indentation
 44â†’
 45â†’Indentation formatting - respects `.editorconfig` `indent_size` with no continuation indent (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more).
 46â†’
 47â†’Rule id: `indent`
 48â†’
 49â†’## Max line length
 50â†’
 51â†’Ensures that lines do not exceed the given length of `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` (see [EditorConfig](../configuration-ktlint/) section for more). This rule does not apply in a number of situations. For example, in the case a line exceeds the maximum line length due to and comment that disables ktlint rules than that comment is being ignored when validating the length of the line. The `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier` can be set to ignore identifiers which are enclosed in backticks, which for example is very useful when you want to allow longer names for unit tests.
 52â†’
 53â†’Rule id: `max-line-length`
 54â†’
 55â†’## Modifier order
 56â†’
 57â†’Consistent order of modifiers
 58â†’
 59â†’Rule id: `modifier-order`
 60â†’
 61â†’## Multiline if-else
 62â†’
 63â†’Braces required for multiline if/else statements.
 64â†’
 65â†’Rule id: `multiline-if-else`
 66â†’
 67â†’## No blank lines before `}`
 68â†’
 69â†’No blank lines before `}`.
 70â†’
 71â†’Rule id: `no-blank-line-before-rbrace`
 72â†’
 73â†’## No blank lines in chained method calls
 74â†’
 75â†’Rule id: `no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`
 76â†’
 77â†’## No consecutive blank lines
 78â†’ 
 79â†’Rule id: `no-consecutive-blank-lines`
 80â†’
 81â†’## No empty (`{}`) class bodies
 82â†’
 83â†’Rule id: `no-empty-class-body`
 84â†’
 85â†’## No leading empty lines in method blocks
 86â†’ 
 87â†’Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-method-block`
 88â†’
 89â†’## No line break after else
 90â†’
 91â†’Disallows line breaks after the else keyword if that could lead to confusion, for example:
 92â†’```kotlin
 93â†’if (conditionA()) {
 94â†’    doSomething()
 95â†’} else
 96â†’if (conditionB()) {
 97â†’    doAnotherThing()
 98â†’}
 99â†’```
100â†’
101â†’Rule id: `no-line-break-after-else`
102â†’
103â†’## No line break before assignment 
104â†’
105â†’When a line is broken at an assignment (`=`) operator the break comes after the symbol.
106â†’
107â†’Rule id: `no-line-break-before-assignment`
108â†’
109â†’## No multi spaces
110â†’
111â†’Except in indentation and in KDoc's it is not allowed to have multiple consecutive spaces.
112â†’
113â†’Rule id: `no-multi-spaces`
114â†’
115â†’## No semicolons
116â†’
117â†’No semicolons (unless used to separate multiple statements on the same line).
118â†’
119â†’Rule id: `no-semi`
120â†’
121â†’## No trailing whitespaces
122â†’
123â†’Rule id: `no-trailing-spaces`
124â†’
125â†’## No `Unit` as return type 
126â†’
127â†’The `Unit` type is not allowed as return type of a function.
128â†’returns (`fun fn {}` instead of `fun fn: Unit {}`)
129â†’
130â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
131â†’
132â†’    ```kotlin
133â†’    fun fn() {}
134â†’    ```
135â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
136â†’
137â†’    ```kotlin
138â†’    fun fn(): Unit {}
139â†’    ```
140â†’
141â†’Rule id: `no-unit-return`
142â†’
143â†’## No unused imports
144â†’
145â†’Rule id: `no-unused-imports`
146â†’
147â†’## No wildcard imports
148â†’
149â†’No wildcard imports except imports listed in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`.
150â†’
151â†’!!! warning
152â†’
153â†’    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:
154â†’    ```editorconfig
155â†’    [*.{kt,kts}]
156â†’    ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = nothing
157â†’    ```
158â†’
159â†’Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports`
160â†’
161â†’## Package name
162â†’
163â†’Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.
164â†’
165â†’Rule id: `package-name`
166â†’
167â†’## Parameter list wrapping
168â†’
169â†’When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line
170â†’
171â†’Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping`
172â†’
173â†’## Parameter wrapping
174â†’
175â†’When a function or class parameter doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
176â†’
177â†’Rule id: `parameter-wrapping`
178â†’
179â†’## Property wrapping
180â†’
181â†’When a property doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
182â†’
183â†’Rule id: `property-wrapping`
184â†’
185â†’## String template
186â†’
187â†’Consistent string templates (`$v` instead of `${v}`, `${p.v}` instead of `${p.v.toString()}`)
188â†’
189â†’Rule id: `string-template`
190â†’
191â†’## Trailing comma on call site
192â†’
193â†’Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194â†’
195â†’!!! important
196â†’    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197â†’
198â†’!!! note
199â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
200â†’    
201â†’    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
202â†’    
203â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
204â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
205â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
206â†’
207â†’!!! note
208â†’    Trailing comma on call site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped calls. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
209â†’
210â†’    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
211â†’
212â†’        ```kotlin
213â†’        FooWrapper(
214â†’            Foo(
215â†’                a = 3,
216â†’                b = 4,
217â†’            ),
218â†’        )
219â†’        ```
220â†’    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
221â†’
222â†’        ```kotlin
223â†’        FooWrapper(Foo(
224â†’            a = 3,
225â†’            b = 4,
226â†’        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
227â†’        ```
228â†’
229â†’
230â†’Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-call-site`
231â†’
232â†’## Trailing comma on declaration site
233â†’
234â†’Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on declaration site.
235â†’
236â†’!!! important
237â†’    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
238â†’
239â†’!!! note
240â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
241â†’
242â†’    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
243â†’    
244â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
245â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
246â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
247â†’
248â†’!!! note
249â†’    Trailing comma on declaration site is automatically disabled if the [Wrapping](#wrapping) rule (or, before version `0.45.0`, the [Indentation](#indentation) rule) is disabled or not loaded. Because it cannot provide proper formatting with unwrapped declarations. (see [dependencies](./dependencies.md)).
250â†’
251â†’    === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
252â†’
253â†’        ```kotlin
254â†’        class FooWrapper(
255â†’            val foo = Foo(
256â†’                a = 3,
257â†’                b = 4,
258â†’            ),
259â†’        )
260â†’        ```
261â†’    === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
262â†’
263â†’        ```kotlin
264â†’        class FooWrapper(val foo = Foo(
265â†’            a = 3,
266â†’            b = 4,
267â†’        ),) // it's weird to insert "," between unwrapped (continued) parenthesis
268â†’        ```
269â†’
270â†’
271â†’Rule id: `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`
272â†’
273â†’## Wrapping
274â†’
275â†’Inserts missing newlines (for example between parentheses of a multi-line function call).
276â†’
277â†’Rule id: `wrapping`
278â†’
279â†’## Spacing
280â†’
281â†’### Annotation spacing
282â†’
283â†’Annotations should be separated by a single line break.
284â†’
285â†’Rule id: `annotation-spacing`
286â†’
287â†’### Colon spacing
288â†’
289â†’Consistent spacing around colon.
290â†’
291â†’Rule id: `colon-spacing`
292â†’
293â†’### Comma spacing
294â†’
295â†’Consistent spacing around comma.
296â†’
297â†’Rule id: `comma-spacing`
298â†’
299â†’### Comment spacing
300â†’
301â†’The end of line comment sign `//` should be preceded and followed by exactly a space.
302â†’
303â†’Rule id: `comment-spacing`
304â†’
305â†’### Curly spacing
306â†’
307â†’Consistent spacing around curly braces.
308â†’
309â†’Rule id: `curly-spacing`
310â†’
311â†’### Dot spacing
312â†’
313â†’Consistent spacing around dots.
314â†’
315â†’Rule id: `dot-spacing`
316â†’
317â†’### Double colon spacing
318â†’
319â†’No spaces around `::`.
320â†’
321â†’Rule id: `double-colon-spacing`
322â†’
323â†’### Keyword spacing
324â†’
325â†’Consistent spacing around keywords.
326â†’
327â†’Rule id: `keyword-spacing`
328â†’
329â†’### Operator spacing
330â†’
331â†’Consistent spacing around operators.
332â†’
333â†’Rule id: `op-spacing`
334â†’
335â†’### Parenthesis spacing
336â†’
337â†’Consistent spacing around parenthesis.
338â†’
339â†’Rule id: `paren-spacing`
340â†’
341â†’### Range spacing
342â†’
343â†’Consistent spacing around range operators.
344â†’
345â†’Rule id: `range-spacing`
346â†’
347â†’### Angle bracket spacing
348â†’
349â†’No spaces around angle brackets when used for typing.
350â†’
351â†’Rule id: `spacing-around-angle-brackets`
352â†’
353â†’### Blank line between declarations with annotations
354â†’
355â†’Declarations with annotations should be separated by a blank line.
356â†’
357â†’Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations`
358â†’
359â†’### Blank line between declaration with comments
360â†’
361â†’Declarations with comments should be separated by a blank line.
362â†’
363â†’Rule id: `spacing-between-declarations-with-comments`
364â†’
365â†’### Unary operator spacing
366â†’
367â†’No spaces around unary operators.
368â†’
369â†’Rule id: `unary-op-spacing`
370â†’

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2â†’
  3â†’!!! danger
  4â†’
  5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6â†’
  7â†’## Code style
  8â†’
  9â†’By default, the `offical` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `android`.
 10â†’
 11â†’```ini
 12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 13â†’ktlint_code_style = official
 14â†’```
 15â†’
 16â†’## Disabled rules
 17â†’
 18â†’!!! note
 19â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 20â†’
 21â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 22â†’
 23â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 24â†’```editorconfig
 25â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 26â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all rules from the `experimental` rule set provided by KtLint
 27â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 28â†’```
 29â†’
 30â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 31â†’```editorconfig
 32â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 33â†’```
 34â†’
 35â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 36â†’```editorconfig
 37â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 38â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 39â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 40â†’```
 41â†’
 42â†’!!! note
 43â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 44â†’
 45â†’
 46â†’## Final newline
 47â†’
 48â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 49â†’
 50â†’```ini
 51â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 52â†’insert_final_newline = true
 53â†’```
 54â†’
 55â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 56â†’
 57â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 58â†’
 59â†’By default, the number of parameters in a function signature is not relevant when rewriting the function signature. Only the maximum line length determines when a function signature should be written on a single line or with multiple lines. Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` can be used, to force a multiline function signature in case the function contain at least a number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `-1` (default) to disable this setting.
 60â†’
 61â†’```ini
 62â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 63â†’ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than= -1
 64â†’```
 65â†’
 66â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 67â†’
 68â†’## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 69â†’
 70â†’Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 71â†’
 72â†’When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 73â†’
 74â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 75â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 76â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 77â†’    .uppercase()
 78â†’
 79â†’// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 80â†’fun someFunction(
 81â†’    a: Any,
 82â†’    b: Any
 83â†’): String = "some-result"
 84â†’    .uppercase()
 85â†’```
 86â†’
 87â†’When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 88â†’
 89â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 90â†’// Given a single line body expression and
 91â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 92â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
 93â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
 94â†’
 95â†’// Given a single line body expression and
 96â†’// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
 97â†’// it does not exceed the max line length
 98â†’fun someFunction(
 99â†’    a: Any,
100â†’    b: Any
101â†’): String = "some-result".uppercase()
102â†’
103â†’// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
104â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
105â†’    "some-result"
106â†’         .uppercase()
107â†’fun someFunction(
108â†’    a: Any,
109â†’    b: Any
110â†’): String =
111â†’    "some-result"
112â†’       .uppercase()
113â†’```
114â†’
115â†’When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
116â†’
117â†’```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
118â†’fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
119â†’    "some-result".uppercase()
120â†’fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
121â†’    a: Any,
122â†’    b: Any
123â†’): String =
124â†’    "some-result"
125â†’        .uppercase()
126â†’```
127â†’
128â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
129â†’
130â†’## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
131â†’
132â†’By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
133â†’
134â†’According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
135â†’
136â†’```kotlin
137â†’@Test
138â†’fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
139â†’    
140â†’}
141â†’```
142â†’
143â†’```ini
144â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
145â†’ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
146â†’```
147â†’
148â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
149â†’
150â†’## Import layouts
151â†’
152â†’By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
153â†’
154â†’The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
155â†’
156â†’*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
157â†’* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
158â†’* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
159â†’
160â†’Examples:
161â†’```kotlin
162â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
163â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
164â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
165â†’```
166â†’
167â†’Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
168â†’
169â†’```ini
170â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
171â†’ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
172â†’```
173â†’
174â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
175â†’
176â†’## Indent size & style
177â†’
178â†’By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level in `official` Kotlin code style while a single tab is used by default in the `android` code style.
179â†’
180â†’```ini
181â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
182â†’indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
183â†’indent_style = space # or "tab"
184â†’```
185â†’
186â†’Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
187â†’
188â†’## Max line length
189â†’
190â†’By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
191â†’
192â†’```ini
193â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
194â†’max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
195â†’```
196â†’
197â†’This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
198â†’
199â†’## Trailing comma on call site
200â†’
201â†’KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
202â†’
203â†’!!! note
204â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
205â†’
206â†’    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
207â†’    
208â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
209â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
210â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
211â†’
212â†’Example:
213â†’```ini
214â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
215â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
216â†’```
217â†’
218â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
219â†’
220â†’## Trailing comma on declaration site
221â†’
222â†’KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
223â†’
224â†’!!! note
225â†’    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
226â†’
227â†’    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
228â†’    
229â†’     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner â€“ as all the focus is on the changed value.
230â†’     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements â€“ there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
231â†’     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
232â†’
233â†’Example:
234â†’```ini
235â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
236â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
237â†’```
238â†’
239â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
240â†’
241â†’## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
242â†’
243â†’You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
244â†’```ini
245â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
246â†’ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
247â†’
248â†’[api/*.{kt,kts}]
249â†’ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
250â†’```
251â†’
252â†’Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.
253â†’

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8â†’
  9â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10â†’- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11â†’- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12â†’- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13â†’
 14â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15â†’
 16â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17â†’
 18â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19â†’* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20â†’* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22â†’
 23â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24â†’
 25â†’#### Experimental rules
 26â†’
 27â†’Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28â†’
 29â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30â†’
 31â†’#### EditorConfig
 32â†’
 33â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34â†’
 35â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36â†’
 37â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38â†’
 39â†’A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40â†’
 41â†’The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42â†’
 43â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44â†’
 45â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46â†’
 47â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48â†’
 49â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50â†’
 51â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

0â†’# Changelog
  1â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  2â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  3â†’
  4â†’## Unreleased
  5â†’
  6â†’WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  7â†’
  8â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
  9â†’- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 10â†’- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 11â†’- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 12â†’
 13â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 14â†’
 15â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 16â†’
 17â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 18â†’* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 19â†’* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 20â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 21â†’
 22â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 23â†’
 24â†’#### Experimental rules
 25â†’
 26â†’Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 27â†’
 28â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 29â†’
 30â†’#### EditorConfig
 31â†’
 32â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 33â†’
 34â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 35â†’
 36â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 37â†’
 38â†’A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 39â†’
 40â†’The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 41â†’
 42â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 43â†’
 44â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 45â†’
 46â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 47â†’
 48â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 49â†’
 50â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 51â†’
 52â†’Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 53â†’
 54â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 55â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 56â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 57â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 58â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 59â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 60â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 61â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 62â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 63â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 64â†’
 65â†’
 66â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 67â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 68â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
 69â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
 70â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
 71â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
 72â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 73â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 74â†’
 75â†’Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
 76â†’
 77â†’Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 78â†’
 79â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
 80â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 81â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2              | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3                   |
 82â†’
 83â†’
 84â†’Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:
 85â†’
 86â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`   | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`     |
 87â†’|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
 88â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtlintVersion   | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.KtlintVersion |
 89â†’
 90â†’Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-logger`:
 91â†’
 92â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`               | New class/package name in `ktlint-logger`                   |
 93â†’|-------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
 94â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt | com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt |
 95â†’
 96â†’Module `ktlint` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli`. Classes below have been relocated in this module:
 97â†’
 98â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli` |
 99â†’|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
100â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.Baseline  | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.api.Baseline  |
101â†’
102â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-baseline` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline`. Classes below have been relocated:
103â†’
104â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-baseline` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` |
105â†’|------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
106â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.baseline               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.baseline               |
107â†’
108â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-checkstyle` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-checkstyle`. Classes below have been relocated:
109â†’
110â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-checkstyle` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-checkstyle` |
111â†’|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
112â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.checkstyle               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.checkstyle               |
113â†’
114â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-format` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-format`. Classes below have been relocated:
115â†’
116â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-format` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-format` |
117â†’|----------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
118â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.format               |
119â†’
120â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-html` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-html`. Classes below have been relocated:
121â†’
122â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-html` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-html` |
123â†’|--------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
124â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.html               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.html               |
125â†’
126â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-json` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-json`. Classes below have been relocated:
127â†’
128â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-json` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-json` |
129â†’|--------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
130â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.json               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.json               |
131â†’
132â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-plain` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain`. Classes below have been relocated:
133â†’
134â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-plain` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain` |
135â†’|---------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
136â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.plain               |
137â†’
138â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-plain-summary` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain-summary`. Classes below have been relocated:
139â†’
140â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-plain-summary` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-plain-summary` |
141â†’|-----------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
142â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain                       | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.plainsummary                |
143â†’
144â†’Module `ktlint-reporter-sarif` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-sarif`. Classes below have been relocated:
145â†’
146â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-sarif` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-sarif` |
147â†’|---------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
148â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.sarif               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.sarif               |
149â†’
150â†’#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
151â†’
152â†’Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
153â†’
154â†’Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
155â†’
156â†’Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
157â†’
158â†’The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
159â†’
160â†’When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
161â†’
162â†’The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
163â†’
164â†’```kotlin
165â†’public class SomeRule : Rule(
166â†’  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
167â†’  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
168â†’) {
169â†’  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
170â†’
171â†’  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
172â†’    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
173â†’  }
174â†’  
175â†’  ...
176â†’}
177â†’```
178â†’
179â†’Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
180â†’
181â†’#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
182â†’
183â†’Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
184â†’
185â†’Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
186â†’
187â†’The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
188â†’
189â†’#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
190â†’
191â†’API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
192â†’
193â†’The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
194â†’
195â†’Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
196â†’
197â†’When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
198â†’
199â†’The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
200â†’
201â†’```kotlin
202â†’public class SomeRule : Rule(
203â†’  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
204â†’  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
205â†’) {
206â†’  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
207â†’
208â†’  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
209â†’    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
210â†’  }
211â†’  
212â†’  ...
213â†’}
214â†’```
215â†’
216â†’Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
217â†’
218â†’Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
219â†’
220â†’#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
221â†’
222â†’Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead.

227â†’* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229â†’* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.

266â†’## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
267â†’
268â†’### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
269â†’
270â†’Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
271â†’
272â†’API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
273â†’
274â†’Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
275â†’
276â†’The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
277â†’```editorconfig
278â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
279â†’ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
280â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
281â†’ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
282â†’```
283â†’Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
284â†’
285â†’Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
286â†’
287â†’From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
288â†’```
289â†’KtLintRuleEngine(
290â†’    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
291â†’    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
292â†’        EditorConfig
293â†’            .builder()
294â†’            .section(
295â†’                Section
296â†’                    .builder()
297â†’                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
298â†’                    .properties(
299â†’                        Property
300â†’                            .builder()
301â†’                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
302â†’                            .value("enabled"),
303â†’                    ),
304â†’            )
305â†’            .build()
306â†’    )
307â†’)
308â†’```
309â†’If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
310â†’
311â†’If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).

347â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
348â†’
349â†’### Indent rule
350â†’
351â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
352â†’
353â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
354â†’
355â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
356â†’
357â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
358â†’
359â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
360â†’
361â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
362â†’
363â†’#### Class relocations
364â†’
365â†’Classes below have been relocated:
366â†’
367â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
368â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
369â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
370â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
371â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
372â†’
373â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
374â†’
375â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
376â†’
377â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
378â†’
379â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
380â†’
381â†’```kotlin
382â†’import java.io.File
383â†’
384â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
385â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
386â†’  // Define configuration
387â†’)
388â†’
389â†’
390â†’// Process a collection of files
391â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
392â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
393â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
394â†’        // Handle lint violations
395â†’    }
396â†’}
397â†’
398â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
399â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
400â†’  code = "code to be linted",
401â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
402â†’) {
403â†’  // Handle lint violations
404â†’}
405â†’```
406â†’
407â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
408â†’
409â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
410â†’
411â†’This API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
412â†’
413â†’Calling this API with a file path results in the `.editorconfig` files that will be accessed when processing that specific file. In case the directory in which the file resides does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
414â†’
415â†’#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
416â†’
417â†’Constant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` is deprecated and will be removed in KtLint version 0.49.0. The file name will be passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
418â†’```kotlin
419â†’if (node.isRoot()) {
420â†’    val fileName = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.name
421â†’    ...
422â†’}
423â†’```
424â†’

473â†’## [0.47.0] - 2022-08-19
474â†’
475â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
476â†’
477â†’If you are not an API consumer nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.
478â†’
479â†’#### Rule lifecycle hooks / deprecate RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier
480â†’
481â†’Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.
482â†’
483â†’The Rule class now offers new life cycle hooks:
484â†’* beforeFirstNode: This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule before processing of nodes starts. The ".editorconfig" properties (including overrides) are provided as parameter.
485â†’* beforeVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST before visiting its child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST. This method is the equivalent of the "visit" life cycle hooks. However, note that in KtLint 0.48, the UserData of the rootnode no longer provides access to the ".editorconfig" properties. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
486â†’* afterVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
487â†’* afterLastNode: This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state of the rule.
488â†’
489â†’Optionally, a rule can stop the traversal of the remainder of the AST whenever the goal of the rule has been achieved. See KDoc on Rule class for more information.
490â†’
491â†’The "visit" life cycle hook will be removed in Ktlint 0.48. In KtLint 0.47 the "visit" life cycle hook will be called *only* when hook "beforeVisitChildNodes" is not overridden. It is recommended to migrate to the new lifecycle hooks in KtLint 0.47. Please create an issue, in case you need additional assistence to implement the new life cycle hooks in your rules.
492â†’
493â†’
494â†’#### Ruleset providing by Custom Rule Set Provider
495â†’
496â†’The KtLint engine needs a more fine-grained control on the instantiation of new Rule instances. Currently, a new instance of a rule can be created only once per file. However, when formatting files the same rule instance is reused for a second processing iteration in case a Lint violation has been autocorrected. By re-using the same rule instance, state of that rule might leak from the first to the second processing iteration.
497â†’
498â†’Providers of custom rule sets have to migrate the custom rule set JAR file. The current RuleSetProvider interface which is implemented in the custom rule set is deprecated and marked for removal in KtLint 0.48. Custom rule sets using the old RuleSetProvider interface will not be run in KtLint 0.48 or above.
499â†’

 62â†’@Command(
 63â†’    headerHeading =
 64â†’    """
 65â†’An anti-bikeshedding Kotlin linter with built-in formatter.
 66â†’(https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint).
 67â†’
 68â†’Usage:
 69â†’  ktlint <flags> [patterns]
 70â†’  java -jar ktlint.jar <flags> [patterns]
 71â†’
 72â†’Examples:
 73â†’  # Check the style of all Kotlin files (ending with '.kt' or '.kts') inside the current dir (recursively).
 74â†’  #
 75â†’  # Hidden folders will be skipped.
 76â†’  ktlint
 77â†’
 78â†’  # Check only certain locations starting from the current directory.
 79â†’  #
 80â†’  # Prepend ! to negate the pattern, KtLint uses .gitignore pattern style syntax.
 81â†’  # Globs are applied starting from the last one.
 82â†’  #
 83â†’  # Hidden folders will be skipped.
 84â†’  # Check all '.kt' files in 'src/' directory, but ignore files ending with 'Test.kt':
 85â†’  ktlint "src/**/*.kt" "!src/**/*Test.kt"
 86â†’  # Check all '.kt' files in 'src/' directory, but ignore 'generated' directory and its subdirectories:
 87â†’  ktlint "src/**/*.kt" "!src/**/generated/**"
 88â†’
 89â†’  # Auto-correct style violations.
 90â†’  ktlint -F "src/**/*.kt"
 91â†’
 92â†’  # Using custom reporter jar and overriding report location
 93â†’  ktlint --reporter=csv,artifact=/path/to/reporter/csv.jar,output=my-custom-report.csv
 94â†’Flags:
 95â†’""",

 97â†’    customSynopsis = [""],

229â†’

231â†’        names = ["--log-level", "-l"],

233â†’        converter = [LogLevelConverter::class],
234â†’    )

236â†’
237â†’    private val tripped = AtomicBoolean()
238â†’    private val fileNumber = AtomicInteger()
239â†’    private val errorNumber = AtomicInteger()
240â†’
241â†’    internal var debug: Boolean = false
242â†’        get() = Level.DEBUG.isGreaterOrEqual(minLogLevel)
243â†’        private set
244â†’
245â†’    private fun disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides() =
246â†’        disabledRules
247â†’            .split(",")
248â†’            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
249â†’            .map {
250â†’                // For backwards compatibility, prefix the rule id with the standard rule set id when missing
251â†’                RuleId.prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(it)
252â†’            }.map { RuleId(it).createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled }
253â†’            .toTypedArray()
254â†’
255â†’    fun run() {
256â†’        val editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride
257â†’            .EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
258â†’            .applyIf(experimental) {
259â†’                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to allow the experimental rule set" }
260â†’                plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
261â†’            }.applyIf(disabledRules.isNotBlank()) {
262â†’                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to disable rules: '$disabledRules'" }
263â†’                plus(*disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides())
264â†’            }.applyIf(android) {
265â†’                logger.debug { "Add editor config override to set code style to 'android'" }
266â†’                plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to CodeStyleValue.android)
267â†’            }.applyIf(stdin) {
268â†’                logger.debug {
269â†’                    "Add editor config override to disable 'filename' rule which can not be used in combination with reading from <stdin>"
270â†’                }
271â†’                plus(FILENAME_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled)
272â†’            }
273â†’
274â†’        if (android) {
275â†’            logger.error {
276â†’                "Option '--android' / '-a' is deprecated and replaced with option '--code-style=android_studio'. Setting '.editorconfig' " +
277â†’                    "property 'ktlint_code_style=android_studio' might be a better idea for a project that is always to formatted with " +
278â†’                    "this code style."
279â†’            }
280â†’        }
281â†’        assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive()
282â†’        patterns = patterns.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty()
283â†’
284â†’        val start = System.currentTimeMillis()
285â†’
286â†’        val ruleProviders = ruleProviders()
287â†’        val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
288â†’            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
289â†’            editorConfigDefaults = editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders),
290â†’            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
291â†’            isInvokedFromCli = true,
292â†’        )
293â†’
294â†’        val baseline = if (stdin || baselinePath.isBlank()) {
295â†’            Baseline(status = Baseline.Status.DISABLED)
296â†’        } else {
297â†’            loadBaseline(baselinePath)
298â†’        }
299â†’
300â†’        val aggregatedReporter = ReporterAggregator(
301â†’            baseline,
302â†’            reporterConfigurations,
303â†’            color,
304â†’            colorName,
305â†’            stdin,
306â†’            format,
307â†’            relative,
308â†’        ).aggregatedReporter()
309â†’
310â†’        aggregatedReporter.beforeAll()
311â†’        if (stdin) {
312â†’            lintStdin(
313â†’                ktLintRuleEngine,
314â†’                aggregatedReporter,
315â†’            )
316â†’        } else {
317â†’            lintFiles(
318â†’                ktLintRuleEngine,
319â†’                baseline.lintErrorsPerFile,
320â†’                aggregatedReporter,
321â†’            )
322â†’        }
323â†’        aggregatedReporter.afterAll()
324â†’
325â†’        logger.debug {
326â†’            "Finished processing in ${System.currentTimeMillis() - start}ms / $fileNumber file(s) scanned / $errorNumber error(s) found"
327â†’        }
328â†’        if (fileNumber.get() == 0) {
329â†’            // Do not return an error as this would implicate that in a multi-module project, each module has to contain
330â†’            // at least one kotlin file.
331â†’            logger.warn { "No files matched $patterns" }
332â†’        }
333â†’        if (tripped.get()) {
334â†’            exitKtLintProcess(1)
335â†’        } else {
336â†’            exitKtLintProcess(0)
337â†’        }
338â†’    }
