340â†’    private fun editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigDefaults {
341â†’        val fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath =
342â†’            editorConfigPath
343â†’                ?.expandTildeToFullPath()
344â†’                ?.let { path -> Paths.get(path) }
345â†’        return EditorConfigDefaults.load(fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath, ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
346â†’    }
347â†’
348â†’    private fun List<String>.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty(): List<String> {
349â†’        val localStdinDelimiter: String? = stdinDelimiter
350â†’        return when {
351â†’            localStdinDelimiter != null -> {
352â†’                val stdinPatterns: Set<String> = readPatternsFromStdin(localStdinDelimiter.ifEmpty { "\u0000" })
353â†’                if (isNotEmpty() && stdinPatterns.isNotEmpty()) {
354â†’                    logger.warn {
355â†’                        "Patterns specified at command line ($this) and patterns from 'stdin' due to flag '--patterns-from-stdin' " +
356â†’                            "($stdinPatterns) are merged"
357â†’                    }
358â†’                }
359â†’                // Note: it is okay in case both the original patterns and the patterns from stdin are empty
360â†’                this.plus(stdinPatterns)
361â†’            }
362â†’            this.isEmpty() -> {
363â†’                logger.info { "Enable default patterns $DEFAULT_PATTERNS" }
364â†’                DEFAULT_PATTERNS
365â†’            }
366â†’            else -> {
367â†’                // Keep original patterns
368â†’                this
369â†’            }
370â†’        }
371â†’    }
372â†’
373â†’    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
374â†’    internal fun ruleProviders(): Set<RuleProvider> = loadRuleProviders(rulesetJarPaths.toFilesURIList())
375â†’
376â†’    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
377â†’    internal fun configureLogger() {
378â†’        logger = KotlinLogging
379â†’            .logger {}
380â†’            .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
381â†’                (logger.underlyingLogger as Logger).level = minLogLevel
382â†’            }
383â†’            .initKtLintKLogger()
384â†’    }
385â†’
386â†’    private fun assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive() {
387â†’        if (stdin && stdinDelimiter != null) {
388â†’            throw ParameterException(
389â†’                commandSpec.commandLine(),
390â†’                "Options --stdin and --patterns-from-stdin are mutually exclusive",
391â†’            )
392â†’        }
393â†’    }
394â†’
395â†’    private fun lintFiles(
396â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
397â†’        lintErrorsPerFile: Map<String, List<KtlintCliError>>,
398â†’        reporter: ReporterV2,
399â†’    ) {
400â†’        FileSystems
401â†’            .getDefault()
402â†’            .fileSequence(patterns)
403â†’            .map { it.toFile() }
404â†’            .takeWhile { errorNumber.get() < limit }
405â†’            .map { file ->
406â†’                val fileName = file.toPath().absolutePathString()
407â†’                Callable {
408â†’                    fileName to process(
409â†’                        ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
410â†’                        code = Code.fromFile(file),
411â†’                        baselineLintErrors = lintErrorsPerFile.getOrDefault(fileName, emptyList()),
412â†’                    )
413â†’                }
414â†’            }.parallel({ (fileName, errList) -> report(Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute, errList, reporter) })
415â†’    }
416â†’
417â†’    private fun lintStdin(
418â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
419â†’        reporter: ReporterV2,
420â†’    ) {
421â†’        report(
422â†’            KtLintRuleEngine.STDIN_FILE,
423â†’            process(
424â†’                ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
425â†’                code = Code.fromStdin(),
426â†’                baselineLintErrors = emptyList(),
427â†’            ),
428â†’            reporter,
429â†’        )
430â†’    }
431â†’
432â†’    private fun report(
433â†’        fileName: String,
434â†’        ktlintCliErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
435â†’        reporter: ReporterV2,
436â†’    ) {
437â†’        fileNumber.incrementAndGet()
438â†’        val errListLimit = minOf(ktlintCliErrors.size, maxOf(limit - errorNumber.get(), 0))
439â†’        errorNumber.addAndGet(errListLimit)
440â†’
441â†’        val relativeRoute = Paths.get(fileName).relativeRoute
442â†’        reporter.before(relativeRoute)
443â†’        ktlintCliErrors
444â†’            .take(errListLimit)
445â†’            .forEach { reporter.onLintError(relativeRoute, it) }
446â†’        reporter.after(relativeRoute)
447â†’    }
448â†’
449â†’    private fun process(
450â†’        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
451â†’        code: Code,
452â†’        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
453â†’    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
454â†’        if (code.fileName != null) {
455â†’            logger.trace { "Checking ${File(code.fileName!!).location(relative)}" }
456â†’        }
457â†’        val result = ArrayList<KtlintCliError>()
458â†’        if (format) {
459â†’            val formattedFileContent = try {
460â†’                ktLintRuleEngine.format(code) { lintError, corrected ->
461â†’                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
462â†’                        line = lintError.line,
463â†’                        col = lintError.col,
464â†’                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
465â†’                        detail = lintError
466â†’                            .detail
467â†’                            .applyIf(corrected) { "$this (cannot be auto-corrected)" },
468â†’                        status = if (corrected) {
469â†’                            FORMAT_IS_AUTOCORRECTED
470â†’                        } else {
471â†’                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
472â†’                        },
473â†’                    )
474â†’                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
475â†’                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
476â†’                        if (!corrected) {
477â†’                            tripped.set(true)
478â†’                        }
479â†’                    }
480â†’                }
481â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
482â†’                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
483â†’                tripped.set(true)
484â†’                code.content // making sure `cat file | ktlint --stdin > file` is (relatively) safe
485â†’            }
486â†’            when {
487â†’                code.isStdIn -> print(formattedFileContent)
488â†’                code.content != formattedFileContent ->
489â†’                    code
490â†’                        .filePath
491â†’                        ?.toFile()
492â†’                        ?.writeText(formattedFileContent, charset("UTF-8"))
493â†’            }
494â†’        } else {
495â†’            try {
496â†’                ktLintRuleEngine.lint(code) { lintError ->
497â†’                    val ktlintCliError = KtlintCliError(
498â†’                        line = lintError.line,
499â†’                        col = lintError.col,
500â†’                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId,
501â†’                        detail = lintError.detail,
502â†’                        status = if (lintError.canBeAutoCorrected) {
503â†’                            LINT_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
504â†’                        } else {
505â†’                            LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
506â†’                        },
507â†’                    )
508â†’                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
509â†’                        result.add(ktlintCliError)
510â†’                        tripped.set(true)
511â†’                    }
512â†’                }
513â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
514â†’                result.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
515â†’                tripped.set(true)
516â†’            }
517â†’        }
518â†’        return result
519â†’    }
520â†’
521â†’    private fun Exception.toKtlintCliError(code: Code): KtlintCliError =
522â†’        this.let { e ->
523â†’            when (e) {
524â†’                is KtLintParseException ->
525â†’                    KtlintCliError(
526â†’                        line = e.line,
527â†’                        col = e.col,
528â†’                        ruleId = "",
529â†’                        detail = "Not a valid Kotlin file (${e.message?.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())})",
530â†’                        status = KOTLIN_PARSE_EXCEPTION,
531â†’                    )
532â†’                is KtLintRuleException -> {
533â†’                    val codeSource = if (code.isStdIn) {
534â†’                        "code"
535â†’                    } else {
536â†’                        "file '${code.fileName}'"
537â†’                    }
538â†’                    logger.debug("Internal Error (${e.ruleId}) in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}", e)
539â†’                    KtlintCliError(
540â†’                        line = e.line,
541â†’                        col = e.col,
542â†’                        ruleId = "",
543â†’                        detail = "Internal Error (rule '${e.ruleId}') in $codeSource at position '${e.line}:${e.col}. " +
544â†’                            "Please create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues " +
545â†’                            "and provide the source code that triggered an error.\n" +
546â†’                            e.stackTraceToString(),
547â†’                        status = KTLINT_RULE_ENGINE_EXCEPTION,
548â†’                    )
549â†’                }
550â†’                else -> throw e
551â†’            }
552â†’        }
553â†’
554â†’    private fun readPatternsFromStdin(delimiter: String): Set<String> {
555â†’        require(delimiter.isNotEmpty())
556â†’
557â†’        return String(System.`in`.readBytes())
558â†’            .split(delimiter)
559â†’            .let { patterns: List<String> ->
560â†’                patterns.filterTo(LinkedHashSet(patterns.size), String::isNotEmpty)
561â†’            }
562â†’    }
563â†’
564â†’    /**
565â†’     * Executes "Callable"s in parallel (lazily).
566â†’     * The results are gathered one-by-one (by `cb(<callable result>)`) in the order of corresponding "Callable"s
567â†’     * in the "Sequence" (think `seq.toList().map { executorService.submit(it) }.forEach { cb(it.get()) }` but without
568â†’     * buffering an entire sequence).
569â†’     *
570â†’     * Once kotlinx-coroutines are out of "experimental" stage everything below can be replaced with
571â†’     * ```
572â†’     * suspend fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(...) {
573â†’     *     val ctx = newFixedThreadPoolContext(numberOfThreads, "Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel")
574â†’     *     ctx.use {
575â†’     *         val channel = produce(ctx, numberOfThreads) {
576â†’     *             for (task in this@parallel) {
577â†’     *                 send(async(ctx) { task.call() })
578â†’     *             }
579â†’     *         }
580â†’     *         for (res in channel) {
581â†’     *             cb(res.await())
582â†’     *         }
583â†’     *     }
584â†’     * }
585â†’     * ```
586â†’     */
587â†’    private fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(
588â†’        cb: (T) -> Unit,
589â†’        numberOfThreads: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors(),
590â†’    ) {
591â†’        val pill = object : Future<T> {
592â†’            override fun isDone(): Boolean {
593â†’                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
594â†’            }
595â†’
596â†’            override fun get(
597â†’                timeout: Long,
598â†’                unit: TimeUnit,
599â†’            ): T {
600â†’                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
601â†’            }
602â†’
603â†’            override fun get(): T {
604â†’                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
605â†’            }
606â†’
607â†’            override fun cancel(mayInterruptIfRunning: Boolean): Boolean {
608â†’                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
609â†’            }
610â†’
611â†’            override fun isCancelled(): Boolean {
612â†’                throw UnsupportedOperationException()
613â†’            }
614â†’        }
615â†’        val q = ArrayBlockingQueue<Future<T>>(numberOfThreads)
616â†’        val producer = thread(start = true) {
617â†’            val executorService = Executors.newCachedThreadPool()
618â†’            try {
619â†’                for (task in this) {
620â†’                    q.put(executorService.submit(task))
621â†’                }
622â†’                q.put(pill)
623â†’            } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
624â†’                // we've been asked to stop consuming sequence
625â†’            } finally {
626â†’                executorService.shutdown()
627â†’            }
628â†’        }
629â†’        try {
630â†’            while (true) {
631â†’                val result = q.take()
632â†’                if (result != pill) cb(result.get()) else break
633â†’            }
634â†’        } finally {
635â†’            producer.interrupt() // in case q.take()/result.get() throws
636â†’            producer.join()
637â†’        }
638â†’    }
639â†’}
640â†’
641â†’private class CodeStyleValueConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<CodeStyleValue> {
642â†’    @Throws(Exception::class)
643â†’    override fun convert(value: String?): CodeStyleValue =
644â†’        when (value?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")) {
645â†’            null -> CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
646â†’            "ktlint_official" -> CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
647â†’            "android_studio" -> CodeStyleValue.android_studio
648â†’            "intellij_idea" -> CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea
649â†’            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid code style value")
650â†’        }
651â†’}
652â†’
653â†’private class LogLevelConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<Level> {
654â†’    @Throws(Exception::class)
655â†’    override fun convert(value: String?): Level =
656â†’        when (value?.uppercase()) {
657â†’            "TRACE" -> Level.TRACE
658â†’            "DEBUG" -> Level.DEBUG
659â†’            "INFO" -> Level.INFO
660â†’            "WARN" -> Level.WARN
661â†’            "ERROR" -> Level.ERROR
662â†’            "NONE" -> Level.OFF
663â†’            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Invalid log level '$value'")
664â†’        }
665â†’}
666â†’
667â†’// Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
668â†’internal fun List<String>.toFilesURIList() =
669â†’    map {
670â†’        val jarFile = File(it.expandTildeToFullPath())
671â†’        if (!jarFile.exists()) {
672â†’            logger.error { "File '$it' does not exist" }
673â†’            exitKtLintProcess(1)
674â†’        }
675â†’        jarFile.toURI().toURL()
676â†’    }
677â†’
678â†’/**
679â†’ * Gets the relative route of the path. Also adjusts the slashes for uniformity between file systems.
680â†’ */
681â†’internal val Path.relativeRoute: String
682â†’    get() {
683â†’        val rootPath = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath()
684â†’        return this
685â†’            .relativeToOrSelf(rootPath)
686â†’            .pathString
687â†’            .replace(File.separatorChar, '/')
688â†’    }
689â†’
690â†’/**
691â†’ * Wrapper around exitProcess which ensure that a proper log line is written which can be used in unit tests for
692â†’ * validating the result of the test.
693â†’ */
694â†’internal fun exitKtLintProcess(status: Int): Nothing {
695â†’    logger.debug { "Exit ktlint with exit code: $status" }
696â†’    exitProcess(status)
697â†’}

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine.Companion.UTF8_BOM
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RuleExecutionRuleFilter
 12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RunAfterRuleFilter
 13â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.ruleRunners
 14â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
 15â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
 17â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 18â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiErrorElement
 19â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.idea.KotlinLanguage
 20â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFile
 21â†’
 22â†’private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 23â†’
 24â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 25â†’
 26â†’internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27â†’    val code: Code,
 28â†’    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29â†’    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30â†’    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31â†’    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32â†’) {
 33â†’    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34â†’
 35â†’    init {
 36â†’        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37â†’    }
 38â†’
 39â†’    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40â†’        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41â†’    }
 42â†’
 43â†’    fun executeRule(
 44â†’        rule: Rule,
 45â†’        fqRuleId: String,
 46â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48â†’    ) {
 49â†’        try {
 50â†’            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51â†’            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52â†’                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53â†’                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54â†’                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55â†’                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56â†’            )
 57â†’            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58â†’            rule.afterLastNode()
 59â†’        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60â†’            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61â†’                e.line,
 62â†’                e.col,
 63â†’                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64â†’                """
 65â†’                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66â†’                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67â†’                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68â†’                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69â†’                """.trimIndent(),
 70â†’                e.cause,
 71â†’            )
 72â†’        }
 73â†’    }
 74â†’
 75â†’    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76â†’        node: ASTNode,
 77â†’        rule: Rule,
 78â†’        fqRuleId: String,
 79â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81â†’    ) {
 82â†’        /**
 83â†’         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84â†’         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85â†’         */
 86â†’        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87â†’        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88â†’            try {
 89â†’                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90â†’                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91â†’                }
 92â†’                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93â†’                    node
 94â†’                        .getChildren(null)
 95â†’                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96â†’                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97â†’                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98â†’                                    childNode,
 99â†’                                    rule,
100â†’                                    fqRuleId,
101â†’                                    autoCorrect,
102â†’                                    emit,
103â†’                                )
104â†’                            }
105â†’                        }
106â†’                }
107â†’                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108â†’                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109â†’                }
110â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
111â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
112â†’                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113â†’                    // original AST
114â†’                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115â†’                        rule,
116â†’                        0,
117â†’                        0,
118â†’                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119â†’                        e.cause ?: e,
120â†’                    )
121â†’                } else {
122â†’                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123â†’                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124â†’                        rule,
125â†’                        line,
126â†’                        col,
127â†’                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128â†’                        e.cause ?: e,
129â†’                    )
130â†’                }
131â†’            }
132â†’        }
133â†’    }
134â†’
135â†’    companion object {
136â†’        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137â†’            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138â†’            code: Code,
139â†’        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140â†’            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141â†’            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142â†’            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143â†’
144â†’            val psiFileName =
145â†’                code.fileName
146â†’                    ?: if (code.script) {
147â†’                        "File.kts"
148â†’                    } else {
149â†’                        "File.kt"
150â†’                    }
151â†’            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152â†’                psiFileName,
153â†’                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154â†’                normalizedText,
155â†’            ) as KtFile
156â†’
157â†’            psiFile
158â†’                .findErrorElement()
159â†’                ?.let { errorElement ->
160â†’                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161â†’                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162â†’                }
163â†’
164â†’            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165â†’
166â†’            val editorConfig =
167â†’                ktLintRuleEngine
168â†’                    .editorConfigLoader
169â†’                    .load(code.filePath)
170â†’                    .also {
171â†’                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172â†’                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173â†’                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174â†’                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175â†’                    }
176â†’
177â†’            val ruleRunners =
178â†’                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179â†’                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180â†’                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181â†’                )
182â†’
183â†’            return RuleExecutionContext(
184â†’                code,
185â†’                rootNode,
186â†’                ruleRunners,
187â†’                editorConfig,
188â†’                positionInTextLocator,
189â†’            )
190â†’        }
191â†’
192â†’        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193â†’            return text
194â†’                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195â†’                .replace("\r", "\n")
196â†’                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197â†’        }
198â†’
199â†’        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200â†’            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201â†’                return this
202â†’            }
203â†’            this.children.forEach { child ->
204â†’                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205â†’                if (errorElement != null) {
206â†’                    return errorElement
207â†’                }
208â†’            }
209â†’            return null
210â†’        }
211â†’    }
212â†’}
213â†’
214â†’private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215â†’    propertyName: String,
216â†’    ktlintVersion: String,
217â†’) {
218â†’    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219â†’        LOGGER.warn {

 22â†’
 23â†’/**
 24â†’ * Import ordering is configured via EditorConfig's property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`, so the Kotlin IDE plugin also recongizes it. Supported values:
 25â†’ * * "*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^" - default IntelliJ IDEA's order, see [IDEA_PATTERN]
 26â†’ * * "*" - alphabetical order as recommended in Android's Kotlin style guide, see [ASCII_PATTERN]
 27â†’ * * custom - defined by the following set of tokens. Tokens can be combined together in a group, groups/tokens must be comma separated:
 28â†’ *  * "*" - wildcard symbol, can be used as follows:
 29â†’ *      1. Single, meaning matching any import (<all other imports> in IDEA)
 30â†’ *      2. After an import path, e.g. "java.*" or "kotlin.io.Closeable.*"
 31â†’ *      3. Doubled after an import path, e.g. "java.**" or "kotlin.io.**", meaning "with subpackages"
 32â†’ *  * "|" - blank line symbol. Only supported single blank lines between imports. Multiple blank lines will be ignored. Blank lines are not allowed outside of import list.
 33â†’ *  * "^" - alias symbol, can be used as follows:
 34â†’ *      1. In front of an import path, meaning matching all alias imports from this path, e.g. "^android.*"
 35â†’ *      2. Alone, meaning matching any alias import - "^" (<all other alias imports> in IDEA)
 36â†’ *  * import paths - these can be full paths, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard paths meaning "with subpackages", e.g. "kotlin.**"
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * In case the custom property is not provided, the rule defaults to alphabetical order in case of "android" flag supplied, or to idea otherwise.
 39â†’ */
 40â†’public class ImportOrderingRule :
 41â†’    StandardRule(
 42â†’        id = "import-ordering",
 43â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY),
 44â†’    ) {
 45â†’    private lateinit var importsLayout: List<PatternEntry>
 46â†’    private lateinit var importSorter: ImportSorter
 47â†’
 48â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49â†’        importsLayout = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY]
 50â†’        importSorter = ImportSorter(importsLayout)
 51â†’    }
 52â†’
 53â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 54â†’        node: ASTNode,
 55â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 56â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 57â†’    ) {
 58â†’        if (node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_LIST) {
 59â†’            val children = node.getChildren(null)
 60â†’            if (children.isNotEmpty()) {
 61â†’                // Get unique imports and blank lines
 62â†’                val (autoCorrectDuplicateImports: Boolean, imports: List<ASTNode>) =
 63â†’                    getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(children, emit)
 64â†’
 65â†’                val hasComments = children.any { it.elementType == ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT || it.elementType == ElementType.EOL_COMMENT }
 66â†’                val sortedImports = imports
 67â†’                    .asSequence()
 68â†’                    .mapNotNull { it.psi as? KtImportDirective } // sorter expects KtImportDirective, whitespaces are inserted afterwards
 69â†’                    .sortedWith(importSorter)
 70â†’                    .map { it.node } // transform back to ASTNode in order to operate over its method (addChild)
 71â†’
 72â†’                // insert blank lines wherever needed
 73â†’                // traverse the list using fold to have previous and current element and decide if the blank line is needed in between
 74â†’                // based on the ImportSorter imported patterns and indexes from the comparator
 75â†’                val sortedImportsWithSpaces = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
 76â†’                sortedImports.fold(null as ASTNode?) { prev, current ->
 77â†’                    val index1 = if (prev == null) -1 else importSorter.findImportIndex((prev.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 78â†’                    val index2 = importSorter.findImportIndex((current.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 79â†’
 80â†’                    var hasBlankLines = false
 81â†’                    for (i in (index1 + 1) until index2) {
 82â†’                        if (importSorter.patterns[i] == PatternEntry.BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) {
 83â†’                            hasBlankLines = true
 84â†’                            break
 85â†’                        }
 86â†’                    }
 87â†’                    if (hasBlankLines) {
 88â†’                        sortedImportsWithSpaces += PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n\n")
 89â†’                    }
 90â†’                    sortedImportsWithSpaces += current
 91â†’
 92â†’                    return@fold current
 93â†’                }
 94â†’
 95â†’                if (hasComments) {
 96â†’                    emit(
 97â†’                        node.startOffset,
 98â†’                        ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE) +
 99â†’                            " -- no autocorrection due to comments in the import list",
100â†’                        false,
101â†’                    )
102â†’                } else {
103â†’                    val autoCorrectWhitespace = hasTooMuchWhitespace(children) && !isCustomLayout()
104â†’                    val autoCorrectSortOrder = !importsAreEqual(imports, sortedImportsWithSpaces)
105â†’                    if (autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace) {
106â†’                        emit(
107â†’                            node.startOffset,
108â†’                            ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE),
109â†’                            true,
110â†’                        )
111â†’                    }
112â†’                    if (autoCorrect && (autoCorrectDuplicateImports || autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace)) {
113â†’                        node.removeRange(node.firstChildNode, node.lastChildNode.treeNext)
114â†’                        sortedImportsWithSpaces.reduce { current, next ->
115â†’                            node.addChild(current, null)
116â†’                            if (current !is PsiWhiteSpace && next !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
117â†’                                node.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n"), null)
118â†’                            }
119â†’                            return@reduce next
120â†’                        }
121â†’                        node.addChild(sortedImportsWithSpaces.last(), null)
122â†’                    }
123â†’                }
124â†’            }
125â†’        }
126â†’    }
127â†’
128â†’    private fun getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(
129â†’        children: Array<ASTNode>,
130â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
131â†’    ): Pair<Boolean, List<ASTNode>> {
132â†’        var autoCorrectDuplicateImports = false
133â†’        val imports = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
134â†’        val importTextSet = mutableSetOf<String>()
135â†’
136â†’        children.forEach { current ->
137â†’            val isPsiWhiteSpace = current.psi is PsiWhiteSpace
138â†’
139â†’            if (current.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE ||
140â†’                isPsiWhiteSpace && current.textLength > 1 // also collect empty lines, that are represented as "\n\n"
141â†’            ) {
142â†’                if (isPsiWhiteSpace || importTextSet.add(current.text)) {
143â†’                    imports += current
144â†’                } else {
145â†’                    emit(
146â†’                        current.startOffset,
147â†’                        "Duplicate '${current.text}' found",
148â†’                        true,
149â†’                    )
150â†’                    autoCorrectDuplicateImports = true
151â†’                }
152â†’            }
153â†’        }
154â†’
155â†’        return autoCorrectDuplicateImports to imports
156â†’    }
157â†’
158â†’    private fun importsAreEqual(
159â†’        actual: List<ASTNode>,
160â†’        expected: List<ASTNode>,
161â†’    ): Boolean {
162â†’        if (actual.size != expected.size) return false
163â†’
164â†’        val combined = actual.zip(expected)
165â†’        return combined.all { (first, second) ->
166â†’            if (first is PsiWhiteSpace && second is PsiWhiteSpace) {
167â†’                return@all (first as PsiWhiteSpace).text == (second as PsiWhiteSpace).text
168â†’            }
169â†’            return@all first == second
170â†’        }
171â†’    }
172â†’
173â†’    private fun isCustomLayout() = importsLayout != IDEA_PATTERN && importsLayout != ASCII_PATTERN
174â†’
175â†’    private fun hasTooMuchWhitespace(nodes: Array<ASTNode>): Boolean {
176â†’        return nodes.any { it is PsiWhiteSpace && (it as PsiWhiteSpace).text != "\n" }
177â†’    }
178â†’
179â†’    public companion object {
180â†’        /**
181â†’         * Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a).
182â†’         * No blank lines between major groups (android, com, junit, net, org, java, javax).
183â†’         * Single group regardless of import type.
184â†’         *
185â†’         * https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#import_statements
186â†’         */
187â†’        private val ASCII_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*")
188â†’
189â†’        /**
190â†’         * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a),
191â†’         * except such groups as "java", "javax" and "kotlin" that are placed in the end. Within the groups the alphabetical order is preserved.
192â†’         * Alias imports are placed in a separate group in the end of the list with alphabetical order inside.
193â†’         * No blank lines between groups.
194â†’         *
195â†’         * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L87-L91
196â†’         */
197â†’        private val IDEA_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^")
198â†’
199â†’        private const val IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between " +
200â†’            "with \"java\", \"javax\", \"kotlin\" and aliases in the end"
201â†’        private const val ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between"
202â†’        private const val CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered according to the pattern specified in .editorconfig"
203â†’
204â†’        private val ERROR_MESSAGES = mapOf(
205â†’            IDEA_PATTERN to IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE,
206â†’            ASCII_PATTERN to ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE,
207â†’        )
208â†’
209â†’        private val EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
210â†’            { _, value ->
211â†’                when {
212â†’                    value.isNullOrBlank() -> PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
213â†’                        value,
214â†’                        "Import layout must contain at least one entry of a wildcard symbol (*)",
215â†’                    )
216â†’                    value == "idea" -> {
217â†’                        LOGGER.warn {
218â†’                            "`idea` is deprecated! Please use `*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE " +
219â†’                                "plugin recognizes the value"
220â†’                        }
221â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
222â†’                            value,
223â†’                            IDEA_PATTERN,
224â†’                        )
225â†’                    }
226â†’                    value == "ascii" -> {
227â†’                        LOGGER.warn {
228â†’                            "`ascii` is deprecated! Please use `*` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE plugin recognizes the value"
229â†’                        }
230â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
231â†’                            value,
232â†’                            ASCII_PATTERN,
233â†’                        )
234â†’                    }
235â†’                    else -> try {
236â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
237â†’                            value,
238â†’                            parseImportsLayout(value),
239â†’                        )
240â†’                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
241â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
242â†’                            value,
243â†’                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
244â†’                        )
245â†’                    }
246â†’                }
247â†’            }
248â†’
249â†’        public val IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
250â†’            EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>>(
251â†’                type = PropertyType(
252â†’                    "ij_kotlin_imports_layout",
253â†’                    "Defines imports order layout for Kotlin files",
254â†’                    EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER,
255â†’                ),
256â†’                defaultValue = IDEA_PATTERN,

258â†’                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
259â†’            )

261â†’}

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering
  2â†’
  3â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.resolve.ImportPath
  4â†’
  5â†’/**
  6â†’ * Represents an entry in the imports layout pattern. Contains matching logic for imports.
  7â†’ *
  8â†’ * Adopted from https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinPackageEntry.kt#L10
  9â†’ */
 10â†’public class PatternEntry(
 11â†’    packageName: String,
 12â†’    public val withSubpackages: Boolean,
 13â†’    public val hasAlias: Boolean,
 14â†’) {
 15â†’    private val packageName = packageName.removeSuffix(".*")
 16â†’
 17â†’    private fun matchesPackageName(otherPackageName: String): Boolean {
 18â†’        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY || this == ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 19â†’        if (this == BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) return false
 20â†’
 21â†’        if (otherPackageName.startsWith(packageName)) {
 22â†’            if (otherPackageName.length == packageName.length) {
 23â†’                return true
 24â†’            }
 25â†’            if (withSubpackages && otherPackageName[packageName.length] == '.') {
 26â†’                return true
 27â†’            }
 28â†’        }
 29â†’        return false
 30â†’    }
 31â†’
 32â†’    internal fun matches(import: ImportPath): Boolean {
 33â†’        return matchesPackageName(import.pathStr.removeSuffix(".*"))
 34â†’    }
 35â†’
 36â†’    internal fun isBetterMatchForPackageThan(
 37â†’        entry: PatternEntry?,
 38â†’        import: ImportPath,
 39â†’    ): Boolean {
 40â†’        if (hasAlias != import.hasAlias() || !matchesPackageName(import.pathStr)) return false
 41â†’        if (entry == null) return true
 42â†’
 43â†’        if (entry.hasAlias != hasAlias) return false
 44â†’        // Any matched package is better than ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY
 45â†’        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return false
 46â†’        if (entry == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 47â†’
 48â†’        if (entry.withSubpackages != withSubpackages) return !withSubpackages
 49â†’
 50â†’        return entry.packageName.count { it == '.' } < packageName.count { it == '.' }
 51â†’    }
 52â†’
 53â†’    override fun toString(): String =
 54â†’        when (this) {
 55â†’            ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> WILDCARD_CHAR
 56â†’            ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> ALIAS_CHAR
 57â†’            else -> "$packageName.$WILDCARD_CHAR" + if (withSubpackages) WILDCARD_CHAR else ""
 58â†’        }
 59â†’
 60â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
 61â†’        if (this === other) return true
 62â†’        if (javaClass != other?.javaClass) return false
 63â†’
 64â†’        other as PatternEntry
 65â†’
 66â†’        if (withSubpackages != other.withSubpackages) return false
 67â†’        if (hasAlias != other.hasAlias) return false
 68â†’        if (packageName != other.packageName) return false
 69â†’
 70â†’        return true
 71â†’    }
 72â†’
 73â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int {
 74â†’        var result = withSubpackages.hashCode()
 75â†’        result = 31 * result + hasAlias.hashCode()
 76â†’        result = 31 * result + packageName.hashCode()
 77â†’        return result
 78â†’    }
 79â†’

 81â†’        val BLANK_LINE_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 82â†’            PatternEntry(
 83â†’                BLANK_LINE_CHAR,
 84â†’                withSubpackages = true,
 85â†’                hasAlias = false,
 86â†’            )
 87â†’
 88â†’        val ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 89â†’            PatternEntry(

 91â†’                withSubpackages = true,

 94â†’
 95â†’        val ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =

 98â†’                withSubpackages = true,

100â†’            )
101â†’    }
102â†’}

content.text:
Summary:
The default exceptions for wildcard imports (`java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`) have been removed for the `ktlint_official` code style in `NoWildcardImportsRule`. This change ensures that wildcard imports from these packages will now trigger a warning when using the official ktlint code style, as requested in issue [#1792](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792).

Changes:
- Modified `NoWildcardImportsRule.kt` to set `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` for the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property.
- Updated `docs/rules/standard.md` to reflect that the default wildcard exceptions are not applied when using the `ktlint_official` code style.
- Updated `docs/rules/configuration-ktlint.md` to clarify the behavior of wildcard imports across different code styles.
- Added a new entry in `CHANGELOG.md` under the `### Fixed` section for the unreleased version.

 386â†’    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 387â†’        // Outer indent context
 388â†’        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
 389â†’            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 390â†’        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 391â†’        startIndentContext(
 392â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 393â†’            toAstNode = rbrace,
 394â†’            firstChildIndent = "",
 395â†’            lastChildIndent = "",
 396â†’        )
 397â†’
 398â†’        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 399â†’        node
 400â†’            .treeParent
 401â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 402â†’            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 403â†’            ?.let { arrow ->
 404â†’                startIndentContext(
 405â†’                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 406â†’                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 407â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 408â†’                )
 409â†’                startIndentContext(
 410â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 411â†’                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 412â†’                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 413â†’                )
 414â†’            }
 415â†’    }

 417â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 418â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 419â†’            // val fieldExample =
 420â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 421â†’            //              paramA,
 422â†’            //              paramB,
 423â†’            //              paramC ->
 424â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 425â†’            //      }
 426â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 427â†’        } else {
 428â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 429â†’            // val fieldExample =
 430â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 431â†’            //                     paramB,
 432â†’            //                     paramC ->
 433â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 434â†’            //     }
 435â†’            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 436â†’                ?.let { callExpression ->
 437â†’                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 438â†’                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 439â†’                            " { "
 440â†’                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 441â†’                }
 442â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 443â†’        }

 446â†’        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 447â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 448â†’            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null

 451â†’        startIndentContext(

 453â†’            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 454â†’            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 455â†’            childIndent = "",
 456â†’        )

 459â†’    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 460â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 461â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 462â†’        node
 463â†’            .findChildByType(EQ)
 464â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 465â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 466â†’                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 467â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 468â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 469â†’            }
 470â†’
 471â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 472â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 473â†’            //     fun process(
 474â†’            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 475â†’            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 476â†’            //     ): List<Output>
 477â†’            node
 478â†’                .findChildByType(COLON)
 479â†’                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 480â†’                    nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 481â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 482â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 483â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 484â†’                }
 485â†’        }
 486â†’
 487â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 488â†’        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 489â†’        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 490â†’            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 491â†’            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 492â†’        ) {
 493â†’            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 494â†’                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 495â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 496â†’            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 497â†’        } else {
 498â†’            startIndentContext(
 499â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 500â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 501â†’                childIndent = "",
 502â†’            )
 503â†’        }
 504â†’    }

 591â†’    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 592â†’        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 593â†’            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 594â†’            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 595â†’            val conditionIndentContext =
 596â†’                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 597â†’            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 598â†’            startIndentContext(
 599â†’                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 600â†’                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 601â†’                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 602â†’                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 603â†’            )
 604â†’            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 605â†’        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 606â†’            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 607â†’        ) {
 608â†’            startIndentContext(node)
 609â†’        }
 610â†’    }

 777â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 778â†’        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 779â†’        node
 780â†’            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
 781â†’            ?.let { finally ->
 782â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 783â†’                    fromAstNode = finally,
 784â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 785â†’                    childIndent = "",
 786â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 787â†’            }
 788â†’
 789â†’        node
 790â†’            .findChildByType(CATCH)
 791â†’            ?.let { catch ->
 792â†’                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 793â†’                    fromAstNode = catch,
 794â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 795â†’                    childIndent = "",
 796â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 797â†’            }
 798â†’
 799â†’        startIndentContext(
 800â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 801â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 802â†’            lastChildIndent = "",
 803â†’        )
 804â†’    }

 806â†’    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 807â†’        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 808â†’        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 809â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
 810â†’                modifierList
 811â†’                    .children()
 812â†’                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 813â†’                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 814â†’            }
 815â†’            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }

 819â†’    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {

 822â†’            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 823â†’        ) {
 824â†’            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()

 826â†’        return fromAstNode!!

 877â†’                    "${indentContext.nodes}"

 880â†’                .removeLast()
 881â†’                .also {

 900â†’    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 901â†’        node: ASTNode,
 902â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 903â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 904â†’    ) {
 905â†’        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 906â†’            return
 907â†’        }
 908â†’
 909â†’        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 910â†’        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 911â†’        val text = node.text
 912â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 913â†’        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 914â†’            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 915â†’                emit(
 916â†’                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 917â†’                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 918â†’                    true,
 919â†’                )
 920â†’            } else {
 921â†’                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 922â†’                // been emitted.
 923â†’            }
 924â†’            LOGGER.trace {
 925â†’                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 926â†’                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 927â†’            }
 928â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 929â†’                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 930â†’                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 931â†’                )
 932â†’            }
 933â†’        } else {
 934â†’            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 935â†’        }
 936â†’    }

 938â†’    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 939â†’        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 940â†’        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 941â†’            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 942â†’            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 943â†’        }
 944â†’
 945â†’        nextLeaf
 946â†’            ?.parent({ it.psi is PsiComment }, strict = false)
 947â†’            ?.let { comment ->
 948â†’                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 949â†’                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 950â†’                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 951â†’                }
 952â†’                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 953â†’                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 954â†’                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 955â†’                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 956â†’                    return true
 957â†’                }
 958â†’            }
 959â†’        return false
 960â†’    }

 962â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""

 967â†’    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
 968â†’        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 969â†’        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 970â†’        val adjustedChildIndent =
 971â†’            when {
 972â†’                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
 973â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
 974â†’                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
 975â†’
 976â†’                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
 977â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
 978â†’                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
 979â†’
 980â†’                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
 981â†’            }

 985â†’    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
 986â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 987â†’        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
 988â†’            SPACE -> {
 989â†’                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
 990â†’                    emit(
 991â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 992â†’                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
 993â†’                        true,
 994â†’                    )
 995â†’                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
 996â†’                } else {
 997â†’                    nodeIndent
 998â†’                }
 999â†’            }
1000â†’
1001â†’            TAB -> {
1002â†’                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1003â†’                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1004â†’                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1005â†’                    emit(
1006â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1007â†’                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1008â†’                        true,
1009â†’                    )
1010â†’                    indentConfig
1011â†’                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1012â†’                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1013â†’                } else {
1014â†’                    nodeIndent
1015â†’                }
1016â†’            }
1017â†’        }
1018â†’    }

1031â†’    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1032â†’        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1033â†’        val acceptableTrailingSpaces = when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1034â†’            KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1035â†’                // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1036â†’                // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1037â†’                KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1038â†’            }
1039â†’
1040â†’            TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1041â†’                // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1042â†’                //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1043â†’                //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1044â†’                TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1045â†’            }
1046â†’
1047â†’            else -> ""
1048â†’        }
1049â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1050â†’        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1051â†’            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1052â†’        } else {
1053â†’            ""
1054â†’        }
1055â†’    }

1072â†’    private data class IndentContext(
1073â†’        /**
1074â†’         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1075â†’         */
1076â†’        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1077â†’

1083â†’
1084â†’        /**

1088â†’        val nodeIndent: String,
1089â†’
1090â†’        /**

1093â†’         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.

1095â†’        val firstChildIndent: String,
1096â†’
1097â†’        /**
1098â†’         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1099â†’         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1100â†’         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1101â†’         */
1102â†’        val childIndent: String,
1103â†’
1104â†’        /**
1105â†’         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1106â†’         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1107â†’         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1108â†’         */
1109â†’        val lastChildIndent: String,
1110â†’
1111â†’        /**
1112â†’         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1113â†’         */
1114â†’        val activated: Boolean = false,
1115â†’    ) {
1116â†’        val nodes: String
1117â†’            get() =
1118â†’                fromASTNode
1119â†’                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1120â†’                    ?.leaves()

1123â†’                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1124â†’                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()

1128â†’
1129â†’        fun indent() =
1130â†’            if (activated) {
1131â†’                nodeIndent + childIndent
1132â†’            } else {
1133â†’                nodeIndent
1134â†’            }
1135â†’
1136â†’        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1137â†’    }

1142â†’private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {

1146â†’        Pair("", "")

1156â†’private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1157â†’    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1158â†’        expectedIndent: String,
1159â†’        node: ASTNode,
1160â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1161â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1162â†’    ) {
1163â†’        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1164â†’        node
1165â†’            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1166â†’            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1167â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1168â†’            ?.let { _ ->
1169â†’                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1170â†’                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1171â†’                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1172â†’                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1173â†’                    emit(
1174â†’                        node.startOffset,
1175â†’                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1176â†’                        false,
1177â†’                    )
1178â†’                    return
1179â†’                }
1180â†’
1181â†’                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1182â†’                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1183â†’                    if (node.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n") {
1184â†’                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1185â†’                        // should have no indent as well.
1186â†’                        ""
1187â†’                    } else {
1188â†’                        expectedIndent
1189â†’                    }
1190â†’                node.children()
1191â†’                    .forEach {
1192â†’                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1193â†’                            (
1194â†’                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1195â†’                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1196â†’                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1197â†’                                )
1198â†’                        ) {
1199â†’                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1200â†’                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1201â†’                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1202â†’                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1203â†’                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1204â†’                                } else {
1205â†’                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1206â†’                                }
1207â†’                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1208â†’                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1209â†’                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1210â†’                                emit(
1211â†’                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1212â†’                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1213â†’                                        "string",
1214â†’                                    true,
1215â†’                                )
1216â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
1217â†’                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1218â†’                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1219â†’                                    )
1220â†’                                }
1221â†’                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1222â†’                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1223â†’                                // the closing quotes.
1224â†’                                emit(
1225â†’                                    it.startOffset,
1226â†’                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1227â†’                                    true,
1228â†’                                )
1229â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
1230â†’                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1231â†’                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1232â†’                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1233â†’                                        )
1234â†’                                    } else {
1235â†’                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1236â†’                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1237â†’                                        )
1238â†’                                    }
1239â†’                                }
1240â†’                            }
1241â†’                        }
1242â†’                    }
1243â†’            }
1244â†’    }
1245â†’
1246â†’    /**
1247â†’     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1248â†’     * the closing quotes.
1249â†’     */
1250â†’    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1251â†’        children()
1252â†’            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1253â†’            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1254â†’            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1255â†’            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1256â†’            .let { indents ->
1257â†’                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote = indents
1258â†’                    .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1259â†’                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1260â†’                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1261â†’                } else {
1262â†’                    indents
1263â†’                }
1264â†’            }
1265â†’            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1266â†’            .minOrNull()
1267â†’            ?: 0
1268â†’
1269â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1270â†’
1271â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")

1273â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1274â†’        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1275â†’            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }

1277â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1278â†’        for (child in node.children()) {
1279â†’            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1280â†’                val v = child.text
1281â†’                if (v == "\n") {

165â†’            //     ) = "some-value"
166â†’            //
167â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
168â†’            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
169â†’            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
170â†’            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
171â†’            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
172â†’            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
173â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
174â†’                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
175â†’                // signature needs to be recalculated
176â†’                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
177â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
178â†’            } else {
179â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
180â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
181â†’            }
182â†’        } else {
183â†’            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
184â†’            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
185â†’            // signature.
186â†’            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature = node
187â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
188â†’                .none { it.textContains('\n') }
189â†’            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
190â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
191â†’            } else {
192â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
193â†’            }
194â†’        }
195â†’    }
196â†’
197â†’    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
198â†’        val closingParenthesis =
199â†’            node
200â†’                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
201â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
202â†’        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature = node
203â†’            .functionSignatureNodes()
204â†’            .childrenBetween(
205â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
206â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { false },
207â†’            )
208â†’
209â†’        return node.indent(false).length +
210â†’            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
211â†’    }
212â†’
213â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine(): Boolean =
214â†’        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
215â†’            ?.children()
216â†’            .orEmpty()
217â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
218â†’            .mapNotNull {
219â†’                // If the value parameter contains a modifier then this list is followed by a white space
220â†’                it.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)?.nextSibling { true }
221â†’            }.any { it.textContains('\n') }
222â†’
223â†’    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
224â†’        node: ASTNode,
225â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
226â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
227â†’    ): Int {
228â†’        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
229â†’
230â†’        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
231â†’        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
232â†’        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
233â†’            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
234â†’            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
235â†’    }

237â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
238â†’
239â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
240â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
241â†’            .joinTextToString()
242â†’            .length
